+    "title": "valkey: [NEW] Atomic slot migration HLD ",
+    "description": "[NEW] Atomic slot migration HLD . Requested by 6+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current valkey setup",
+        "description": "Verify your valkey version and configuration:\n```bash\nvalkey-server --version\n```\nThis feature requires a working valkey installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review valkey configuration",
+        "description": "Review the relevant valkey configuration:\n**Last Updated:  Jan 12, 2025**\n\n# Problem Statement\n\nEfficient slot migration is critical for maintaining the scalability and resilience of Valkey clusters, but the current client-driven migration approach presents significant challenges."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for [NEW] Atomic slot migration HLD ",
+        "description": "and I discussed another option (in the context of atomic slot migration) which essentially implements slot-level replication. Here is the high level flow\n\n1. source (parent) forks a child with a set of slots to migrate\n2. child streams slots to be migrated in the RDB format (same as replication)\n3.\n```yaml\nvalkey-cli -h 10.0.0.3 -p 6379\n> HELLO 3\n> CLUSTER IMPORT SLOTS 1000 2000 \nc7e98d63-5717-479f-8b8a-4c1d49720286 \n> CLUSTER IMPORT STATUS OPID c7e98d63-5717-479f-8b8a-4c1d49720286 \n1# \"requested-slots\" => (integer) 1001 \n2# \"completed-slots\" => (integer) 800 \n3# \"failed-slots\" => (integer) 50 \n4# \"importing-slots\" => (integer) 15 \n5# \"canceled-slots\" => (integer) 0 \n> CLUSTER IMPORT CANCEL OPID c7e98d63-5717-479f-8b8a-4c1d49720286\n +OK \n> CLUSTER IMPORT STATUS OPID c7e98d63-5717-479f-8b8a-4c1d49720286 \n1# \"requested-slots\" => (integer) 1001 \n2# \"completed-slots\" => (integer) 800 \n3# \"failed-slo\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected.\nConfirm the feature described in \"[NEW] Atomic slot migration HLD \" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "and I discussed another option (in the context of atomic slot migration) which essentially implements slot-level replication. Here is the high level flow\n\n1. source (parent) forks a child with a set of slots to migrate\n2. child streams slots to be migrated in the RDB format (same as replication)\n3. target needs to support no-blocking load of the RDB stream (think of co-routines)\n4.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "valkey-cli -h 10.0.0.3 -p 6379\n> HELLO 3\n> CLUSTER IMPORT SLOTS 1000 2000 \nc7e98d63-5717-479f-8b8a-4c1d49720286 \n> CLUSTER IMPORT STATUS OPID c7e98d63-5717-479f-8b8a-4c1d49720286 \n1# \"requested-slots\" => (integer) 1001 \n2# \"completed-slots\" => (integer) 800 \n3# \"failed-slots\" => (integer) 50 \n4# \"importing-slots\" => (integer) 15 \n5# \"canceled-slots\" => (integer) 0 \n> CLUSTER IMPORT CANCEL OPID c7e98d63-5717-479f-8b8a-4c1d49720286\n +OK \n> CLUSTER IMPORT STATUS OPID c7e98d63-5717-479f-8b8a-4c1d49720286 \n1# \"requested-slots\" => (integer) 1001 \n2# \"completed-slots\" => (integer) 800 \n3# \"failed-slots\" => (integer) 50 \n4# \"importing-slots\" => (integer) 0 \n5# \"canceled-slots\" => (integer) 151",
+        "struct rdbPushLoadState {\n    size_t id;\n    char *buff;\n    size_t buf_size;\n    robj * curr;\n    int curr_rdb_type;\n    size_t curr_remaining_elements;\n    mstime_t deadline;\n}"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
